title: Brief
description: The matcher -> writer handoff artifact (spec §9.2): slot ref, matched archetype, deterministic pre-seed, target length/format, and writing-framework reference. The optional enrichment field-set (enricher -> writer, RD-13) rides on the same brief; its ABSENCE must validate — the matcher -> writer contract works without it.

content_id.description: Content id this brief belongs to (keys the per-stage workspace artifacts).

format.description: Content format within the platform (platform-descriptor-defined set).

mode.description: Publish posture inherited from the originating operator command.

slot_ref.description: Calendar slot this brief produces for (a calendar slot_id).

archetype.description: Matched content archetype id (matcher's slot->archetype decision).

pillar.description: Content pillar / strategic lane (freeform plain text).

theme.description: Theme or topic for the content (freeform plain text).

pre_seed.description: The deterministic pre-seed: the angle plus drafting direction the matcher hands the writer.

angle.description: The core argument / angle for the content.

must_not_include.description: Elements the draft must avoid (e.g. emojis, hashtags, links, CTA).

target_length.description: Target length guidance for the draft (e.g. a character range or word band). Freeform; the platform gate enforces hard limits.

framework_ref.description: Repo-relative reference to the per-length writing framework the writer seat boot-reads for this brief (spec §9.3).

media_decision_hint.description: Optional pre-seed media sourcing hint (e.g. reuse / modify / generate / capture-needed). The media seat owns the final decision.

enrichment.description: OPTIONAL enricher -> writer field-set (RD-13). Absence validates: the matcher -> writer contract does not require it. When present, carries the enrichment packet that sits between matcher and writer.

summary.description: Enrichment summary: the strongest framing for the content.

proof_stack.description: Supporting proof for the argument, separated into primary, supporting, and fact-safety guidance.

fact_safety.description: Claims the draft must not make (unverified facts, private details).

core_tension.description: The central tension the content plays on.

reader_takeaway.description: What the reader should take away.

variant_lanes.description: Distinct argument lanes for the writer's variants. Each lane is a separate argument, not a rewrite.

comparator_transfer.description: A comparator reference and the mechanic to borrow, with explicit do-not-copy guardrails. The comparator ref is a Zone-U reference id and carries no drafted text.

humanizer_notes.description: Style/anti-pattern guidance to keep the copy human (e.g. avoid filler, forced slogans).
